我先

美國大選︱「造票」「舞弊」指控瘋傳 《蘋果》拆解五大不實指控

憑藉後期點算的郵遞選票，拜登在美國大選重拾上風，在總統特朗普帶頭質疑選舉點票的公正性下，網上過去半日瘋傳不少針對選舉不公的指控，不少是缺乏根據、甚至捏造，但香港的社交媒體及論壇今日仍充斥類似訊息。《蘋果》抽出其中幾個最為廣傳的指控，拆解並釐清當中的事實。

傳聞一：拜登在密歇根州「神奇增加」13萬票
點票截圖顯示，拜登在密歇根州的票數由1,992,356票急增至2,130,695票，「神奇地」連續取得138,339票，其間特朗普一票都拿不到，令特朗普優勢大幅縮減。
來源：
有美國Twitter用戶在《紐約時報》等美國傳媒選舉網站截圖，經右翼媒體《The Federalist》創辦人Sean Davis及共和黨人Matt Mackowiak轉發後廣傳。

拆解：
有關講法不實。
有關截圖的票數的確曾經短暫出現在傳媒的網頁，不過原因是密歇根州當局向無黨派選舉新聞分析服務商Decision Desk HQ（DDHQ）提供了一個錯誤的票數檔案，由於DDHQ不會更改官方提供的票數檔案，而多間傳媒都透過DDHQ實時更新自己的開票網站，導致錯誤直接反映在不同傳媒網站。密歇根州當局發現錯誤後已立刻更正。
雖然錯誤曾經出現，但有關貼文亦有造假之嫌。錯誤數字是密州Shiawassee縣的票數，特朗普在該縣以23,154票對15,371票擊敗拜登，不過點票人員錯誤地在拜登票數多輸入一個「0」，變成153,710。不過，截圖中顯示拜登票數的變化並非153,710，而是138,339，因為截圖顯示的其實是拜登票數在當局修正錯誤前後變化，2,130,695票是誤加票數後的總數，當局修正後，拜登的票數減去錯誤票數多出來的部分，即153,710減15,371，剛好就是138,339票。
換言之，138,339票其實是修正後拜登票數的跌幅，亦解釋了為何特朗普的票數在截圖中沒有變動；至於截圖者則是將兩個數字前後調轉，再用文字營造拜登票數急增的效果。了解事件原由後，曾轉發傳聞的共和黨人Matt Mackowiak已刪除貼文。

傳聞二：多個州分超過100%投票率
網上流傳一幅圖表，顯示內華達、賓夕法尼亞、明尼蘇達等7個搖擺州分都錄得超過100%的投票率，引來種票的質疑。
來源：
來源不明，不過根據Google圖片搜尋，此圖主要出現在中文網站，最早見於簡體網站「看帖神器」kantie.org 轄下的「北美華人網」、一個以「作弊證據已經出來了」為題的帖文。

拆解：
有關傳聞不實。
圖中顯示的登記選民數字明顯並非今年最新的登記選民數字，以內華達州為例，根據圖中引述的 worldpopulationreview.com 網站，今年的登記選民數字應為1,821,356。資料顯示，圖中的「# registered」應為2018年的估計登記選民數字，因此全部都四捨五入至千位數；至於「Projected votes（預測票數）」一欄的數字則未能考究來源，但與今屆各州的預測票數均不吻合。

傳聞三：大量特朗普「選票」被撕碎丟棄
網上廣傳一條片段，畫面以中文寫上「賓州，撕碎丟棄的都是投川普的票…」字眼，片中一名男子手持一張印有特朗普照片的紙張，地上則佈滿撕毀的紙碎。
來源：
標上中文字句的片段來源不明；相關片段早於10月初見於一個支持特朗普的Facebook專頁「USA Headline News」。

拆解：
有關傳聞不實。
首先，片中的紙張根本並非選票，而是特朗普呼籲支持者申請郵遞選票的表格，片中可見紙張上寫上「Pennsylvania Application for Mail-in Ballot」，背面有「President Trump wants you to return this form」的字樣和特朗普的照片，手持紙張的男子亦稱它是一張「賓州郵遞選票的表格」。
另外，現時廣傳的片段長15秒，是經剪輯的片段，最早見於「USA Headline News」的片段原本長55秒，在該片清楚可見現場是一個運載廢紙的環保斗，美國傳媒《今日美國》曾向印製表格的公司確認，片中出現的只是該公司印刷剩餘的廢料。

傳聞四：選民收到預先填了拜登的選票
網上流傳片段，片中男子手持一份選了拜登及其他民主黨候選人的選票，指有居於紐約皇后區的居民聲稱自己收到一份預先已經選了民主黨的選票，質疑選票是由機器預先填好。
來源：
拍攝片段的美國自由身記者Jake Novak，他在10月29日上載片段，之後獲特朗普兒子Eric Trump轉發，片段至今都仍在社交媒體流傳。

拆解：
有關講法已獲澄清。
Novak拒絕透露向他提供有關選票的人的身份，但指選票是屬於一個8個月前搬離他的住址的人。根據核實網站「 Factcheck.org 」，片段廣傳後，紐約市選舉委員會主動根據片中的選民編號，聯絡到索取該選票的選民，確認現時在加州的他收到的是一張空白選票。詢問下發現，該選民填妥選票後，未有依規定在選票封套外再用信封裝妥就直接寄出，導致選票被發還到他於紐約的地址，現居於上址的居民就收到一份已填的選票。

傳聞五：拜登在威斯康辛州「造票」致選票急增
網上廣傳一幅選舉分析網站FiveThirtyEight的圖表，顯示拜登在威斯康辛州的得票，在美國時間4日凌晨突然「跳級」急增，引來「灌票（ballot dump）」舞弊的質疑，特朗普亦響應稱自己的優勢「神奇地消失」。
來源：
原圖來自選舉分析網站FiveThirtyEight，用以解釋選情；保守派網站「Gateway Pundit」是其中一個最早轉發圖表並稱之為「舞弊」的來源。

美國大選︱「造票」「舞弊」指控瘋傳 《蘋果》拆解五大不實指控

憑藉後期點算的郵遞選票，拜登在美國大選重拾上風，在總統特朗普帶頭質疑選舉點票的公正性下，網上過去半日瘋傳不少針對選舉不公的指控，不少是缺乏根據、甚至捏造，但香港的社交媒體及論壇今日仍充斥類似訊息。《蘋果》抽出其中幾個最為廣傳的指控，拆解並釐清當中的事實。

傳聞一：拜登在密歇根州「神奇增加」13萬票
點票截圖顯示，拜登在密歇根州的票數由1,992,356票急增至2,130,695票，「神奇地」連續取得138,339票，其間特朗普一票都拿不到，令特朗普優勢大幅縮減。
來源：
有美國Twitter用戶在《紐約時報》等美國傳媒選舉網站截圖，經右翼媒體《The Federalist》創辦人Sean Davis及共和黨人Matt Mackowiak轉發後廣傳。

拆解：
有關講法不實。
有關截圖的票數的確曾經短暫出現在傳媒的網頁，不過原因是密歇根州當局向無黨派選舉新聞分析服務商Decision Desk HQ（DDHQ）提供了一個錯誤的票數檔案，由於DDHQ不會更改官方提供的票數檔案，而多間傳媒都透過DDHQ實時更新自己的開票網站，導致錯誤直接反映在不同傳媒網站。密歇根州當局發現錯誤後已立刻更正。
雖然錯誤曾經出現，但有關貼文亦有造假之嫌。錯誤數字是密州Shiawassee縣的票數，特朗普在該縣以23,154票對15,371票擊敗拜登，不過點票人員錯誤地在拜登票數多輸入一個「0」，變成153,710。不過，截圖中顯示拜登票數的變化並非153,710，而是138,339，因為截圖顯示的其實是拜登票數在當局修正錯誤前後變化，2,130,695票是誤加票數後的總數，當局修正後，拜登的票數減去錯誤票數多出來的部分，即153,710減15,371，剛好就是138,339票。
換言之，138,339票其實是修正後拜登票數的跌幅，亦解釋了為何特朗普的票數在截圖中沒有變動；至於截圖者則是將兩個數字前後調轉，再用文字營造拜登票數急增的效果。了解事件原由後，曾轉發傳聞的共和黨人Matt Mackowiak已刪除貼文。

Dominion系統舞弊指控

爬梳完主流、左派、右翼、全國性、地方性、非營利等媒體的相關資料後，我認為密西根州安特里姆縣、乃至全國的Dominion系統，在這次大選並沒有被用來舞弊。原因有以下幾個：

1. 並非全美都採用Dominion投票系統。根據保守派媒體華盛頓郵報報導，10個搖擺州中有351個郡縣使用Dominion，而川普贏了其中的八成郡縣。若Dominion真的存在舞弊，川普怎麼還能拿下八成？

2. 由紅翻藍的五州中，僅密西根州及喬治亞州普遍使用Dominion，亞利桑那州、威斯康辛州、賓州都只有少量郡縣採用。既然使用的郡縣這麼少，Dominion如何能左右選情？

3. 全郡縣使用Dominion的路易斯安那州，最後結果是川普勝出。若Dominion真的存在舞弊，為何路易斯安那州不是拜登拿下？

4. 2008、2012甚至2016年的大選，Dominion早已在多州使用，當時為何無人質疑它具有舞弊的嫌疑？僅因當時贏的是川普？

5. 大規模選舉舞弊是非常困難的，更何況是在選舉機制並不統一、各州甚至各郡縣自行其事的美國。除了下圖多種投票設備以外，整個計票過程的機制正如一名密西根大學研究所學生所描述，遠比讀者想像來得嚴謹。

在這個最講自由人權的國家、網路通訊技術最發達的時代，發生得同時買通這麼多利害關係方、了解多州投票制度缺陷，還懂得破解不同類型投票機器的舞弊，機率能有多高？

6. 此份鑑識報告所附證據，並無法直接證明票數遭竄改或是發生重大舞弊，而且我十分同意投票系統專家Ryan Macias所反駁的重點──此份報告撰寫者並不熟悉投票系統的架構與運作，因此報告中充滿了由想像產生的誤解，甚至可以說是先射箭再畫靶。一份由不懂投票系統的資安專家所撰寫的報告，公信力有多少？

7. 安特里姆縣最後在兩黨選務人員的見證下，人工重新計算選票，最後得票數與先前Dominion開票結果十分接近，川普從9748票增加了11票，拜登由5960票減少了1票。如果真的有舞弊，有人傻到只灌一票？

Nonstop ballot counting
The viral surveillance footage was recorded at Atlanta’s State Farm Arena, which was used as a polling site last month. In the video, taken in the days after the Nov. 3 presidential election, site workers, reporters and poll watchers can be seen leaving as others begin packing ballots into containers, which were inaccurately dubbed suitcases.

The video of the activities of the poll workers is not doctored or significantly altered. Insinuations that workers were packing away these ballots for nefarious purposes, however, are incorrect.

In reality, the workers were not placing the ballots in suitcases, but their proper storage containers. They were doing this because they thought their work was done for the day, and they could leave.

“What you saw – the secret suitcase with magic ballots – were actually ballots that had been packed into those absentee ballot carriers by the workers in the plain view of the monitors and the press,” Georgia Election System Implementation Manager Gabriel Sterling said, according to the Augusta Chronicle.

“They were under the misbegotten impression that they were getting to go home,” he continued. “They had been there all day long. They were tired and they thought they could go home.”

Learning the Fulton workers were planning to leave, Georgia Secretary of State Brad Raffensperger made certain they did not.

Related claims that the ballots were somehow violated or made otherwise invalid in the process are unfounded. The actions on video are part of the routine ballot opening process.

“We’ve had our investigators watch all, many, several hours of it yesterday and what essentially happened is, and we knew about part of this on election night itself, around 10:15-10:20, there's two groups of people in this room that are working," Sterling said during a Newsmax interview. "There are cutters, the people who are opening the envelopes, and there's the ones who are scanning.”

At such a late hour, the "cutters" had already finished opening the ballots, leaving only the scanning process to be conducted. This meant that less workers were needed on site for the remaining ballots.

In the wake of claims about the footage, Georgia election officials and local media monitored hours of footage from the arena to determine if there had been any wrongdoing. Neither party found cases of fraud from the incident.

4. 2008、2012甚至2016年的大選，Dominion早已在多州使用，當時為何無人質疑它具有舞弊的嫌疑？僅因當時贏的是川普？

5. 大規模選舉舞弊是非常困難的，更何況是在選舉機制並不統一、各州甚至各郡縣自行其事的美國。除了下圖多種投票設備以外，整個計票過程的機制正如一名密西根大學研究所學生所描述，遠比讀者想像來得嚴謹。

在這個最講自由人權的國家、網路通訊技術最發達的時代，發生得同時買通這麼多利害關係方、了解多州投票制度缺陷，還懂得破解不同類型投票機器的舞弊，機率能有多高？

6. 此份鑑識報告所附證據，並無法直接證明票數遭竄改或是發生重大舞弊，而且我十分同意投票系統專家Ryan Macias所反駁的重點──此份報告撰寫者並不熟悉投票系統的架構與運作，因此報告中充滿了由想像產生的誤解，甚至可以說是先射箭再畫靶。一份由不懂投票系統的資安專家所撰寫的報告，公信力有多少？

7. 安特里姆縣最後在兩黨選務人員的見證下，人工重新計算選票，最後得票數與先前Dominion開票結果十分接近，川普從9748票增加了11票，拜登由5960票減少了1票。如果真的有舞弊，有人傻到只灌一票？

Dominion系統舞弊指控

爬梳完主流、左派、右翼、全國性、地方性、非營利等媒體的相關資料後，我認為密西根州安特里姆縣、乃至全國的Dominion系統，在這次大選並沒有被用來舞弊。原因有以下幾個：

1. 並非全美都採用Dominion投票系統。根據保守派媒體華盛頓郵報報導，10個搖擺州中有351個郡縣使用Dominion，而川普贏了其中的八成郡縣。若Dominion真的存在舞弊，川普怎麼還能拿下八成？

2. 由紅翻藍的五州中，僅密西根州及喬治亞州普遍使用Dominion，亞利桑那州、威斯康辛州、賓州都只有少量郡縣採用。既然使用的郡縣這麼少，Dominion如何能左右選情？

3. 全郡縣使用Dominion的路易斯安那州，最後結果是川普勝出。若Dominion真的存在舞弊，為何路易斯安那州不是拜登拿下？

4. 2008、2012甚至2016年的大選，Dominion早已在多州使用，當時為何無人質疑它具有舞弊的嫌疑？僅因當時贏的是川普？

5. 大規模選舉舞弊是非常困難的，更何況是在選舉機制並不統一、各州甚至各郡縣自行其事的美國。除了下圖多種投票設備以外，整個計票過程的機制正如一名密西根大學研究所學生所描述，遠比讀者想像來得嚴謹。

在這個最講自由人權的國家、網路通訊技術最發達的時代，發生得同時買通這麼多利害關係方、了解多州投票制度缺陷，還懂得破解不同類型投票機器的舞弊，機率能有多高？

6. 此份鑑識報告所附證據，並無法直接證明票數遭竄改或是發生重大舞弊，而且我十分同意投票系統專家Ryan Macias所反駁的重點──此份報告撰寫者並不熟悉投票系統的架構與運作，因此報告中充滿了由想像產生的誤解，甚至可以說是先射箭再畫靶。一份由不懂投票系統的資安專家所撰寫的報告，公信力有多少？

7. 安特里姆縣最後在兩黨選務人員的見證下，人工重新計算選票，最後得票數與先前Dominion開票結果十分接近，川普從9748票增加了11票，拜登由5960票減少了1票。如果真的有舞弊，有人傻到只灌一票？

Nonstop ballot counting
The viral surveillance footage was recorded at Atlanta’s State Farm Arena, which was used as a polling site last month. In the video, taken in the days after the Nov. 3 presidential election, site workers, reporters and poll watchers can be seen leaving as others begin packing ballots into containers, which were inaccurately dubbed suitcases.

The video of the activities of the poll workers is not doctored or significantly altered. Insinuations that workers were packing away these ballots for nefarious purposes, however, are incorrect.

In reality, the workers were not placing the ballots in suitcases, but their proper storage containers. They were doing this because they thought their work was done for the day, and they could leave.

“What you saw – the secret suitcase with magic ballots – were actually ballots that had been packed into those absentee ballot carriers by the workers in the plain view of the monitors and the press,” Georgia Election System Implementation Manager Gabriel Sterling said, according to the Augusta Chronicle.

“They were under the misbegotten impression that they were getting to go home,” he continued. “They had been there all day long. They were tired and they thought they could go home.”

Learning the Fulton workers were planning to leave, Georgia Secretary of State Brad Raffensperger made certain they did not.

Related claims that the ballots were somehow violated or made otherwise invalid in the process are unfounded. The actions on video are part of the routine ballot opening process.

“We’ve had our investigators watch all, many, several hours of it yesterday and what essentially happened is, and we knew about part of this on election night itself, around 10:15-10:20, there's two groups of people in this room that are working," Sterling said during a Newsmax interview. "There are cutters, the people who are opening the envelopes, and there's the ones who are scanning.”

At such a late hour, the "cutters" had already finished opening the ballots, leaving only the scanning process to be conducted. This meant that less workers were needed on site for the remaining ballots.

In the wake of claims about the footage, Georgia election officials and local media monitored hours of footage from the arena to determine if there had been any wrongdoing. Neither party found cases of fraud from the incident.

Nonstop ballot counting
The viral surveillance footage was recorded at Atlanta’s State Farm Arena, which was used as a polling site last month. In the video, taken in the days after the Nov. 3 presidential election, site workers, reporters and poll watchers can be seen leaving as others begin packing ballots into containers, which were inaccurately dubbed suitcases.

The video of the activities of the poll workers is not doctored or significantly altered. Insinuations that workers were packing away these ballots for nefarious purposes, however, are incorrect.

In reality, the workers were not placing the ballots in suitcases, but their proper storage containers. They were doing this because they thought their work was done for the day, and they could leave.

“What you saw – the secret suitcase with magic ballots – were actually ballots that had been packed into those absentee ballot carriers by the workers in the plain view of the monitors and the press,” Georgia Election System Implementation Manager Gabriel Sterling said, according to the Augusta Chronicle.

“They were under the misbegotten impression that they were getting to go home,” he continued. “They had been there all day long. They were tired and they thought they could go home.”

Learning the Fulton workers were planning to leave, Georgia Secretary of State Brad Raffensperger made certain they did not.

Related claims that the ballots were somehow violated or made otherwise invalid in the process are unfounded. The actions on video are part of the routine ballot opening process.

“We’ve had our investigators watch all, many, several hours of it yesterday and what essentially happened is, and we knew about part of this on election night itself, around 10:15-10:20, there's two groups of people in this room that are working," Sterling said during a Newsmax interview. "There are cutters, the people who are opening the envelopes, and there's the ones who are scanning.”

At such a late hour, the "cutters" had already finished opening the ballots, leaving only the scanning process to be conducted. This meant that less workers were needed on site for the remaining ballots.

In the wake of claims about the footage, Georgia election officials and local media monitored hours of footage from the arena to determine if there had been any wrongdoing. Neither party found cases of fraud from the incident.

The term itself might cause a bit of confusion. They are, in most every sense, the same thing.

Some headlines use "mail-in voting" because it more simply describes the process and immediately makes sense. "Absentee" simply means you are absent at the physical poll (somewhat oddly, Georgia also uses the term "absentee voting in-person" to mean what's commonly referred to as early voting.)

In any case, the process is simple: The request form is pre-populated with the voter's name and street address, along with the return mailing address of the county voting office they need to return the application to.

It should be filled out and signed, then affixed with a stamp and returned to the registration office noted on the mailer.

In the event a voter does not have a stamp, they are permitted to scan or take a picture of the completed and signed application and email it to the address provided on the application. The email address is different for each county.

An absentee ballot will then be mailed to the voter for them to complete and return.

The term itself might cause a bit of confusion. They are, in most every sense, the same thing.

Some headlines use "mail-in voting" because it more simply describes the process and immediately makes sense. "Absentee" simply means you are absent at the physical poll (somewhat oddly, Georgia also uses the term "absentee voting in-person" to mean what's commonly referred to as early voting.)

In any case, the process is simple: The request form is pre-populated with the voter's name and street address, along with the return mailing address of the county voting office they need to return the application to.

It should be filled out and signed, then affixed with a stamp and returned to the registration office noted on the mailer.

In the event a voter does not have a stamp, they are permitted to scan or take a picture of the completed and signed application and email it to the address provided on the application. The email address is different for each county.

An absentee ballot will then be mailed to the voter for them to complete and return.

作家曹長青11月26日在Facebook貼出網站「The Gateway Pundit」的文章，引述美國總統特朗普（Donald Trump）的私人律師朱利亞尼（Rudy Giuliani）在賓夕凡尼亞州（Pennsylvania）參議員聽證會上的言論，宣稱是次美國總統選舉期間，該州「發出180萬郵寄票，回收250萬」。[1][2]作家汪浩的Facebook上亦貼出類似消息，至今有近500次分享。[3]

根據語音轉換文字網站Rev.com的記錄，朱利亞尼在聽證會中多次提到「寄出180萬選票，點算了250萬票」的說法[4]，當中更有明確數字，例如在第31分10秒中他指當局寄出1,823,148張缺席或郵寄選票（absentee or mail-in ballots），收回大約140萬張，但在總統選舉投票中點出2,589,242票。

賓夕凡尼亞州官方網站的非正式選舉結果包括郵寄選票數據，當中顯示拜登（Joe Biden）取得1,995,691張、特朗普取得595,538票、另一候選人佐贊臣（Jo Jorgensen）取得24,783票，三人合共得票為2,616,012。換言之，該州今屆美國總統選舉中有超過260萬張郵寄選票。[5]

佛羅里達大學（University of Florida）政治科學教授麥當勞（Michael McDonald）的「美國選舉計劃」（U.S. Elections Project）數據顯示，賓夕凡尼亞州是次選舉共有3,088,123申請郵寄選票，最終有2,629,183張選票寄回。[6]

此外，《美聯社》（Associated Press）在10月28日（投票日前約一星期）的報導同樣指出該州已有逾300萬人申請郵寄選票，並指該州數據顯示各縣已收回約190萬張選票。[7]而在11月3日（投票日）該州州長及州務卿發出的新聞稿指出，當時該州已有超過250萬張郵寄選票。[8]

以上三組數字均反駁了朱利亞尼宣稱「寄出180萬選票，收回約140萬張」的說法。

雖然朱利亞尼未有明確提到其資料來源，但他提供的詳細數字有助找回出處，從而確認其說法錯誤。

在Google搜尋「pennsylvania mail in ballot data」，可以找到賓夕凡尼亞州政府的開放數據網站記錄了今年初選——而非總統選舉——的郵寄選票申請數據，當中顯示共有1,823,148宗申請，跟朱利亞尼提到的數字完全相同。而利用該網站的數據可視化工具，可發現當中有約146萬張選票寄回，同樣跟朱利亞尼提到的數字吻合。[9]

由此可以判斷，朱利亞尼宣稱賓夕凡尼亞當局在美國總統選舉中寄出1,823,148張選票及收回約140萬張選票，實際上是看錯了網站顯示的初選數據。參考賓夕凡尼亞州官方數字，是次大選總共寄出超過300萬張選票，最終點算了超過260萬張郵寄選票，並無出現朱利亞尼所提及的落差。

咁都唔算FC?

作家曹長青11月26日在Facebook貼出網站「The Gateway Pundit」的文章，引述美國總統特朗普（Donald Trump）的私人律師朱利亞尼（Rudy Giuliani）在賓夕凡尼亞州（Pennsylvania）參議員聽證會上的言論，宣稱是次美國總統選舉期間，該州「發出180萬郵寄票，回收250萬」。[1][2]作家汪浩的Facebook上亦貼出類似消息，至今有近500次分享。[3]

根據語音轉換文字網站Rev.com的記錄，朱利亞尼在聽證會中多次提到「寄出180萬選票，點算了250萬票」的說法[4]，當中更有明確數字，例如在第31分10秒中他指當局寄出1,823,148張缺席或郵寄選票（absentee or mail-in ballots），收回大約140萬張，但在總統選舉投票中點出2,589,242票。

賓夕凡尼亞州官方網站的非正式選舉結果包括郵寄選票數據，當中顯示拜登（Joe Biden）取得1,995,691張、特朗普取得595,538票、另一候選人佐贊臣（Jo Jorgensen）取得24,783票，三人合共得票為2,616,012。換言之，該州今屆美國總統選舉中有超過260萬張郵寄選票。[5]

佛羅里達大學（University of Florida）政治科學教授麥當勞（Michael McDonald）的「美國選舉計劃」（U.S. Elections Project）數據顯示，賓夕凡尼亞州是次選舉共有3,088,123申請郵寄選票，最終有2,629,183張選票寄回。[6]

此外，《美聯社》（Associated Press）在10月28日（投票日前約一星期）的報導同樣指出該州已有逾300萬人申請郵寄選票，並指該州數據顯示各縣已收回約190萬張選票。[7]而在11月3日（投票日）該州州長及州務卿發出的新聞稿指出，當時該州已有超過250萬張郵寄選票。[8]

以上三組數字均反駁了朱利亞尼宣稱「寄出180萬選票，收回約140萬張」的說法。

雖然朱利亞尼未有明確提到其資料來源，但他提供的詳細數字有助找回出處，從而確認其說法錯誤。

在Google搜尋「pennsylvania mail in ballot data」，可以找到賓夕凡尼亞州政府的開放數據網站記錄了今年初選——而非總統選舉——的郵寄選票申請數據，當中顯示共有1,823,148宗申請，跟朱利亞尼提到的數字完全相同。而利用該網站的數據可視化工具，可發現當中有約146萬張選票寄回，同樣跟朱利亞尼提到的數字吻合。[9]

由此可以判斷，朱利亞尼宣稱賓夕凡尼亞當局在美國總統選舉中寄出1,823,148張選票及收回約140萬張選票，實際上是看錯了網站顯示的初選數據。參考賓夕凡尼亞州官方數字，是次大選總共寄出超過300萬張選票，最終點算了超過260萬張郵寄選票，並無出現朱利亞尼所提及的落差。

Nonstop ballot counting
The viral surveillance footage was recorded at Atlanta’s State Farm Arena, which was used as a polling site last month. In the video, taken in the days after the Nov. 3 presidential election, site workers, reporters and poll watchers can be seen leaving as others begin packing ballots into containers, which were inaccurately dubbed suitcases.

The video of the activities of the poll workers is not doctored or significantly altered. Insinuations that workers were packing away these ballots for nefarious purposes, however, are incorrect.

In reality, the workers were not placing the ballots in suitcases, but their proper storage containers. They were doing this because they thought their work was done for the day, and they could leave.

“What you saw – the secret suitcase with magic ballots – were actually ballots that had been packed into those absentee ballot carriers by the workers in the plain view of the monitors and the press,” Georgia Election System Implementation Manager Gabriel Sterling said, according to the Augusta Chronicle.

“They were under the misbegotten impression that they were getting to go home,” he continued. “They had been there all day long. They were tired and they thought they could go home.”

Learning the Fulton workers were planning to leave, Georgia Secretary of State Brad Raffensperger made certain they did not.

Related claims that the ballots were somehow violated or made otherwise invalid in the process are unfounded. The actions on video are part of the routine ballot opening process.

“We’ve had our investigators watch all, many, several hours of it yesterday and what essentially happened is, and we knew about part of this on election night itself, around 10:15-10:20, there's two groups of people in this room that are working," Sterling said during a Newsmax interview. "There are cutters, the people who are opening the envelopes, and there's the ones who are scanning.”

At such a late hour, the "cutters" had already finished opening the ballots, leaving only the scanning process to be conducted. This meant that less workers were needed on site for the remaining ballots.

In the wake of claims about the footage, Georgia election officials and local media monitored hours of footage from the arena to determine if there had been any wrongdoing. Neither party found cases of fraud from the incident.

作家曹長青11月26日在Facebook貼出網站「The Gateway Pundit」的文章，引述美國總統特朗普（Donald Trump）的私人律師朱利亞尼（Rudy Giuliani）在賓夕凡尼亞州（Pennsylvania）參議員聽證會上的言論，宣稱是次美國總統選舉期間，該州「發出180萬郵寄票，回收250萬」。[1][2]作家汪浩的Facebook上亦貼出類似消息，至今有近500次分享。[3]

根據語音轉換文字網站Rev.com的記錄，朱利亞尼在聽證會中多次提到「寄出180萬選票，點算了250萬票」的說法[4]，當中更有明確數字，例如在第31分10秒中他指當局寄出1,823,148張缺席或郵寄選票（absentee or mail-in ballots），收回大約140萬張，但在總統選舉投票中點出2,589,242票。

賓夕凡尼亞州官方網站的非正式選舉結果包括郵寄選票數據，當中顯示拜登（Joe Biden）取得1,995,691張、特朗普取得595,538票、另一候選人佐贊臣（Jo Jorgensen）取得24,783票，三人合共得票為2,616,012。換言之，該州今屆美國總統選舉中有超過260萬張郵寄選票。[5]

佛羅里達大學（University of Florida）政治科學教授麥當勞（Michael McDonald）的「美國選舉計劃」（U.S. Elections Project）數據顯示，賓夕凡尼亞州是次選舉共有3,088,123申請郵寄選票，最終有2,629,183張選票寄回。[6]

此外，《美聯社》（Associated Press）在10月28日（投票日前約一星期）的報導同樣指出該州已有逾300萬人申請郵寄選票，並指該州數據顯示各縣已收回約190萬張選票。[7]而在11月3日（投票日）該州州長及州務卿發出的新聞稿指出，當時該州已有超過250萬張郵寄選票。[8]

以上三組數字均反駁了朱利亞尼宣稱「寄出180萬選票，收回約140萬張」的說法。

雖然朱利亞尼未有明確提到其資料來源，但他提供的詳細數字有助找回出處，從而確認其說法錯誤。

在Google搜尋「pennsylvania mail in ballot data」，可以找到賓夕凡尼亞州政府的開放數據網站記錄了今年初選——而非總統選舉——的郵寄選票申請數據，當中顯示共有1,823,148宗申請，跟朱利亞尼提到的數字完全相同。而利用該網站的數據可視化工具，可發現當中有約146萬張選票寄回，同樣跟朱利亞尼提到的數字吻合。[9]

由此可以判斷，朱利亞尼宣稱賓夕凡尼亞當局在美國總統選舉中寄出1,823,148張選票及收回約140萬張選票，實際上是看錯了網站顯示的初選數據。參考賓夕凡尼亞州官方數字，是次大選總共寄出超過300萬張選票，最終點算了超過260萬張郵寄選票，並無出現朱利亞尼所提及的落差。

美國大選︱「造票」「舞弊」指控瘋傳 《蘋果》拆解五大不實指控

憑藉後期點算的郵遞選票，拜登在美國大選重拾上風，在總統特朗普帶頭質疑選舉點票的公正性下，網上過去半日瘋傳不少針對選舉不公的指控，不少是缺乏根據、甚至捏造，但香港的社交媒體及論壇今日仍充斥類似訊息。《蘋果》抽出其中幾個最為廣傳的指控，拆解並釐清當中的事實。

傳聞一：拜登在密歇根州「神奇增加」13萬票
點票截圖顯示，拜登在密歇根州的票數由1,992,356票急增至2,130,695票，「神奇地」連續取得138,339票，其間特朗普一票都拿不到，令特朗普優勢大幅縮減。
來源：
有美國Twitter用戶在《紐約時報》等美國傳媒選舉網站截圖，經右翼媒體《The Federalist》創辦人Sean Davis及共和黨人Matt Mackowiak轉發後廣傳。

拆解：
有關講法不實。
有關截圖的票數的確曾經短暫出現在傳媒的網頁，不過原因是密歇根州當局向無黨派選舉新聞分析服務商Decision Desk HQ（DDHQ）提供了一個錯誤的票數檔案，由於DDHQ不會更改官方提供的票數檔案，而多間傳媒都透過DDHQ實時更新自己的開票網站，導致錯誤直接反映在不同傳媒網站。密歇根州當局發現錯誤後已立刻更正。
雖然錯誤曾經出現，但有關貼文亦有造假之嫌。錯誤數字是密州Shiawassee縣的票數，特朗普在該縣以23,154票對15,371票擊敗拜登，不過點票人員錯誤地在拜登票數多輸入一個「0」，變成153,710。不過，截圖中顯示拜登票數的變化並非153,710，而是138,339，因為截圖顯示的其實是拜登票數在當局修正錯誤前後變化，2,130,695票是誤加票數後的總數，當局修正後，拜登的票數減去錯誤票數多出來的部分，即153,710減15,371，剛好就是138,339票。
換言之，138,339票其實是修正後拜登票數的跌幅，亦解釋了為何特朗普的票數在截圖中沒有變動；至於截圖者則是將兩個數字前後調轉，再用文字營造拜登票數急增的效果。了解事件原由後，曾轉發傳聞的共和黨人Matt Mackowiak已刪除貼文。

Dominion系統舞弊指控

爬梳完主流、左派、右翼、全國性、地方性、非營利等媒體的相關資料後，我認為密西根州安特里姆縣、乃至全國的Dominion系統，在這次大選並沒有被用來舞弊。原因有以下幾個：

1. 並非全美都採用Dominion投票系統。根據保守派媒體華盛頓郵報報導，10個搖擺州中有351個郡縣使用Dominion，而川普贏了其中的八成郡縣。若Dominion真的存在舞弊，川普怎麼還能拿下八成？

2. 由紅翻藍的五州中，僅密西根州及喬治亞州普遍使用Dominion，亞利桑那州、威斯康辛州、賓州都只有少量郡縣採用。既然使用的郡縣這麼少，Dominion如何能左右選情？

3. 全郡縣使用Dominion的路易斯安那州，最後結果是川普勝出。若Dominion真的存在舞弊，為何路易斯安那州不是拜登拿下？

4. 2008、2012甚至2016年的大選，Dominion早已在多州使用，當時為何無人質疑它具有舞弊的嫌疑？僅因當時贏的是川普？

5. 大規模選舉舞弊是非常困難的，更何況是在選舉機制並不統一、各州甚至各郡縣自行其事的美國。除了下圖多種投票設備以外，整個計票過程的機制正如一名密西根大學研究所學生所描述，遠比讀者想像來得嚴謹。

在這個最講自由人權的國家、網路通訊技術最發達的時代，發生得同時買通這麼多利害關係方、了解多州投票制度缺陷，還懂得破解不同類型投票機器的舞弊，機率能有多高？

6. 此份鑑識報告所附證據，並無法直接證明票數遭竄改或是發生重大舞弊，而且我十分同意投票系統專家Ryan Macias所反駁的重點──此份報告撰寫者並不熟悉投票系統的架構與運作，因此報告中充滿了由想像產生的誤解，甚至可以說是先射箭再畫靶。一份由不懂投票系統的資安專家所撰寫的報告，公信力有多少？

7. 安特里姆縣最後在兩黨選務人員的見證下，人工重新計算選票，最後得票數與先前Dominion開票結果十分接近，川普從9748票增加了11票，拜登由5960票減少了1票。如果真的有舞弊，有人傻到只灌一票？
